notes: The doctrinally load-bearing terms are vyavasāyātmikā buddhi (the resolved-by-determination intelligence), 'ekā iha' (one — here, on this path), and 'bahu-śākhā ... avyavasāyinām' (many-branched — of the irresolute). The engineering layer preserves vyavasāya in its precise sense: not 'any decision' but conviction following prior groundwork — Shankara's 'right pramāṇa anchoring' and Ramanuja's 'prior determination of ātma-yāthātmya' are both reflected in the falsifiability clause ('vyavasāya is decision-after-discrimination, not decision-against-discrimination'). The 'iha' (here, on the path of buddhi-yoga just opened) is preserved as the scope marker. The contrast between Shankara (Yoga-of-Knowledge anchoring) and Ramanuja (mokṣa-aṅga acts) is preserved in the disagreement entry without being conflated. The engineering layer does not invert or weaken the Sanskrit's claim. PASS.
